steps.title: Recognize that angles K and L are complementary
steps.content: In a right triangle (angle J = 90°), the two acute angles must sum to 90°. Here, K + L = 71° + 19° = 90°.

steps.title: Apply the cofunction identity
steps.content: Since K and L are complementary: $\sin K = \cos(90° - K) = \cos L$. Therefore $\cos L = \sin K$, which is choice B — B is correct.
